PPTX, PDF39,630 views

Idiomatic expressions

The document discusses idiomatic expressions, which are informal phrases that have meanings different from their literal definitions. It notes that idioms are influenced by culture and use figurative language. Examples are given of common idioms and students are assigned homework that involves identifying idioms, defining their meanings and origins, and incorporating them into written responses.

Idiomatic expressions

  • 1.
    What will youdo if your friend told you to ‘bring the house down’?
  • 2.
    You heard thejudge of a reality show, say that the contestant showed ‘crocodile tears’. How can humans have ‘crocodile tears’?
  • 3.
    You don’t knowhow to dance, but your romantic partner told you that ‘It take two to tango’. Are you really going to perform a dance?
  • 4.
    IDIOMATIC EXPRESSIONS
  • 5.
    IDIOMATIC EXPRESSIONS are a typeof informal English
  • 6.
    IDIOMS has different meaning fromthe literal meaning of the words in the expressions IDIOMATIC EXPRESSIONS
  • 7.
    IDIOMS Uses the figurative language IDIOMATIC EXPRESSIONS
  • 8.
    IDIOMS Influenced by culture ofits origin IDIOMATIC EXPRESSIONS
  • 9.
    IDIOMS SEAT WORK#1 With a partner answer the online activities, posted in edmodo.
  • 10.
    Look for ashort article related to your field of study that used idiomatic expressions. Give the meaning of the idiomatic expressions and research on their origin. Construct a meaningful sentence using the idiomatic expressions. IDIOMS SEAT WORK #2
  • 11.
     On a½ crosswise, respond on the prompt of your choice given below. Use 5 idiomatic expressions in your composition and underline them. A. Write a short narrative about your experiences in the first few weeks in FEU-EAC. Use idiomatic expressions B. Express your opinion on the recent territorial disputes/ issues between the Philippines and China C. Explain your stand on on-line gaming habit of teenagers IDIOMS HOMEWORK
